core_model.structure_summary: Minimization of a composite loss over learned vector representations of graph nodes, combining a noise-contrastive term with log-sigmoid scoring of representation inner products and squared-error reconstruction terms, with representations produced by attention-based aggregations of random-walk statistics.
core_model.canonical_form: \min\ \mathcal{L} = -\sum_{(i,j)\in D}\big[p_{ij}\ln\sigma(\gamma_i^{\top}\tilde\gamma_j/\tau) + Q n_j \ln\sigma(-\gamma_i^{\top}\tilde\gamma_j/\tau)\big] + \sum \lVert \rho - \hat\rho \rVert_2^2,\quad Z = \mathrm{softmax}\!\left(\frac{\tilde Q\tilde K^{\top}}{\sqrt{d}}\right)\tilde V

variables.meaning: composite contrastive plus reconstruction loss to be minimized
attention weight matrices, temperature, negative-sampling factor, random projection
random-walk sampling and Gaussian random-feature projection

deterministic_skeleton.summary: The stationarity condition of the contrastive objective, giving an algebraic fixed-point relation in which the optimal inner-product score equals a log-ratio of co-occurrence to negative-sampling frequency.

notes: Composite deep-learning model (attention over random-walk statistics jointly optimizing two embedding types). The central formal object is the noise-contrastive optimization objective; the paper itself derives its stationarity condition z_ij = ln p_ij - ln(Q n_j), a pointwise-mutual-information-style fixed point, which is taken as the deterministic skeleton. Many moving parts (multiple attention units, reconstruction regularizers) lower confidence in a single canonical core.
